title: Intense Argentinian Beef Empanadas
description: Pungent, dense, meal-prep friendly empanadas made with lean ground beef, rendered fat, and a savory spiced filling, perfect for a quick dinner or packed lunches.
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium heat and add rendered beef fat; once melted, add the finely chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and diced red bell pepper, cooking until the pepper softens, about 3 minutes.
Add the lean ground beef and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned and no longer pink, about 8 minutes.
Stir in tomato paste, paprika, cayenne pepper, oregano, and a pinch of salt and pepper, allowing the mixture to become fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Pour in beef broth and bring the filling to a simmer, letting it reduce until thick and flavorful, about 10 minutes.
Remove the skillet from heat and let the mixture cool for 10 minutes, then transfer to a mixing bowl.
Prepare a flat dough by mixing gluten-free flour with a pinch of salt and adding a beaten egg and a splash of water, then knead until a smooth dough forms.
Divide the dough into 12 equal portions and roll each into a thin circle, about 10 cm in diameter, using a rolling pin.
Spoon 2 tablespoons of the cooled beef filling onto the center of each dough circle, fold over to create a half-moon shape, and seal the edges by pressing with a fork.
Place the filled empanadas on a parchment-lined baking sheet, brush the tops lightly with olive oil, and bake in a preheated oven at 200C for 18 minutes or until golden brown.
Let the empanadas cool for 5 minutes before serving or storing; they keep refrigerated for up to 4 days and can be reheated in a toaster oven.
